us-politics.headline: Two US Warplanes Down Over Iran, Crew Member Missing
us-politics.body:
Two US warplanes were shot down over Iran, with a search underway for at least one missing crew member. The incident marks a sharp escalation in the ongoing US-Iran conflict and has rattled public confidence - roughly half of Americans now expect regional stability to worsen, according to a new survey.

Trump's wartime rhetoric has drawn scrutiny from analysts who say it differs meaningfully from past presidents in tone and legal framing. Critics argue the language blurs lines around congressional war powers. Supporters say it projects strength. Meanwhile, a Democrat slammed prediction market Polymarket for taking bets on the fate of the missing pilot, calling it exploitative.

sources.title: Two U.S. warplanes shot down; search ongoing in Iran for 1 missing crew member
How Trump's wartime rhetoric differs from past presidents and why that matters
Democrat rips Polymarket for taking bets on fate of US pilot shot down over Iran
About half expect worsening stability in Middle East: Survey

us-politics.headline: Army Chief Ousted, Hegseth Faces Bipartisan Pressure
us-politics.body:
Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth's removal of the Army chief of staff drew rare praise for the ousted general from Republican lawmakers, signaling unease within the party over Hegseth's leadership style. The episode adds to what multiple outlets describe as a turbulent week for the administration at home and abroad.

Separately, bipartisan senators are probing meetings between Kremlin-linked figures and US officials, raising questions about diplomatic channels with Russia. Todd Blanche has taken over as the Justice Department's top official, stepping into a role carrying heavy expectations from the White House. Pam Bondi's departure as attorney general has already prompted speculation about her successor.

sources.title: GOP praise pours in for Army chief of staff ousted by Hegseth
President Trump's rocky week at home and abroad
Bipartisan senators probe Kremlin-linked delegation's meetings with US officials
Todd Blanche takes on DOJ’s top job and the burden of Trump’s expectations
Pam Bondi is out as AG — here are the contenders who could replace her

us-politics.headline: Trump Mail-In Ballot Order Triggers Lawsuits, Budget Sparks Fights
us-politics.body:
Democratic-led states filed suit against Trump's executive order seeking to change mail-in voting rules, arguing it oversteps federal authority. The order would impose new documentation requirements that critics say will suppress turnout. The White House frames it as a fraud-prevention measure.

Trump's budget proposal landed with equal controversy. Democrats called it 'bleak and unacceptable,' objecting to cuts across multiple agencies including the TSA, which the plan would partially privatize at smaller airports. The budget also requests $152 million to reopen Alcatraz as a high-security federal prison. Vice President Vance has been tapped to lead a new anti-fraud initiative targeting what the administration describes as taxpayer theft in Democratic-run states.

sources.title: Democratic-led states sue over Trump mail-in ballot order
Trump issues order attempting to change rules for mail-in voting
Democrats fume at White House budget request: ‘Bleak and unacceptable’
Trump budget would cut TSA funding, require privatization at small airports
Trump seeks $152M to rebuild Alcatraz as ‘state-of-the-art secure prison’
Vance tapped as ‘fraud czar’ as Trump targets blue states over taxpayer theft

us-politics.headline: Alito Hospitalized, FBI Breach Declared Major Incident
us-politics.body:
The Supreme Court confirmed Justice Samuel Alito was treated for dehydration at a hospital following a March 20 event, an incident the court had not previously disclosed. Alito reportedly felt ill during a public appearance and was examined on-site before being taken for further care. His health status and court schedule remain under watch.

Elsewhere in Washington, the FBI notified Congress that a data breach affecting a surveillance system has been classified as a major incident. Few details have been released publicly. A former Air Force master sergeant separately pleaded guilty in a $37 million bid-rigging scheme, adding to a string of military-linked legal cases drawing congressional attention.

sources.title: Justice Alito ‘felt ill’ at March event, was examined by doctor, court says
Supreme Court confirms Alito fell ill at March event
Supreme Court Justice Alito treated for dehydration at hospital in previously undisclosed March 20 incident
FBI labels data breach ‘major incident,’ notifies Congress
Ex-Air Force master sergeant pleads guilty in $37M bid-rigging scheme

au-politics.headline: Iran War Reshapes Australian Politics and Global Alliances
au-politics.body:
The US war with Iran is forcing a reckoning in Canberra. Prime Minister Albanese faces pressure to articulate a sharper foreign policy response as the conflict disrupts global energy markets, strands 20,000 sailors in the Gulf, and pushes US allies like the Philippines into economic crisis. World leaders including Macron and Germany's Merz are openly distancing themselves from Trump's approach.

At home, the war's economic ripple effects are landing hard. Energy Minister Chris Bowen is defending the government's handling of a fuel crisis already changing how Australians travel and live. The Guardian reports both Albanese and opposition leader Taylor are scrambling to match voter expectations on cost-of-living pressures tied directly to the conflict.

The crisis is also raising questions about Australia's strategic posture in the Pacific, where communities in Guam are divided over a growing US military presence.

sources.title: World loses patience with Trump as war becomes about more than Iran
Live: Two US planes shot down in Iran, 2 crew members rescued with one still missing
Energy minister Chris Bowen on the impact of the fuel crisis - podcast
Trump’s Iran war has woken Albanese up to a new reality. Will it spur him towards ‘ambitious’ reforms?
The 20,000 sailors who have become sitting ducks in the Gulf
How the Iran war is pushing US ally the Philippines into economic crisis
Strategically critical Pacific island divided over growing US military presence
Another 'lockdown': How long-term caravanners are dealing with the fuel crisis

au-politics.headline: SA Liberals Cling to Opposition Status After One Nation Surge
au-politics.body:
South Australia's Liberal Party is fighting to retain its formal opposition status after One Nation's strong showing left the party's numbers in doubt. Analysts describe the situation as having 'so few precedents' that the outcome remains genuinely uncertain, with the Liberals potentially losing the institutional resources and platform that come with being the recognised opposition.

The result reflects a broader pattern of minor party growth squeezing the major parties from the right. One Nation's gains in South Australia add to a string of results nationally that have complicated the Coalition's path back to power. How the Liberals respond - whether by hardening their position or seeking accommodation with minor parties - will shape the conservative side of politics heading into the next federal cycle.

sources.title: 'So few precedents': One Nation leaves SA Liberals clinging to opposition status

au-politics.headline: Tax Policy Divides Parties as Election Pressure Mounts
au-politics.body:
Both Labor and the Coalition are under pressure to spell out credible tax policies, with voters increasingly focused on who pays what in a high-cost environment. The Guardian reports that even senior Liberal figure Andrew Hastie has suggested miners could contribute more - a notable break from traditional Coalition orthodoxy that signals how much the political ground has shifted.

Albanese is navigating competing demands: progressive voters want bolder redistribution, while the government is wary of spooking business during an already volatile economic period. Taylor faces the reverse problem, needing to offer relief to cost-of-living pressured households without abandoning fiscal conservatism. Neither side has yet landed a clear, differentiated position.

sources.title: Taxing times for Albanese and Taylor as parties seek to match voter expectations

au-politics.headline: Fuel Crisis Bites as Cyclone Narelle Leaves Historic Mark
au-politics.body:
Australia's fuel crisis is deepening, with long-term caravanners describing the experience as another lockdown as high costs force them to abandon travel plans. Energy Minister Bowen has acknowledged the impact but faces criticism that the government's response has been too slow given the crisis's roots in the Iran conflict.

Separately, Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle has been confirmed as a historic storm, with new reporting detailing the destructive path it carved across Australia. The combination of energy disruption and extreme weather is testing household budgets and emergency infrastructure simultaneously. Daylight saving also ends this weekend across most of Australia, a small but practical shift affecting millions.

sources.title: Another 'lockdown': How long-term caravanners are dealing with the fuel crisis
How nightmare Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle became a historic storm
Your pocket guide to daylight savings ending in Australia
Energy minister Chris Bowen on the impact of the fuel crisis - podcast

world.headline: US-Iran War Escalates as Jets Downed, Hormuz Reopens
world.body:
Iran claims it shot down two US fighter jets, with a search underway for at least one pilot. The strikes follow a US-Israeli attack on an Iranian bridge, with survivors describing the assault, while the WHO reports more than 20 attacks on Iranian healthcare facilities since March 1. A Red Crescent warehouse in southwest Iran was also hit.

Despite the fighting, shipping is cautiously resuming. Omani, French, and Japanese vessels transited the Strait of Hormuz, the first commercial traffic since the war began. A UAE gas facility at Habshan suffered one fatality after an intercepted attack, signalling the conflict is spilling into the Gulf. Trump meanwhile told reporters the US could eventually "take the oil" in Iran.

sources.title: Will force be used to reopen Strait of Hormuz?
Survivors of Iran bridge attack recall terror of US-Israeli strike
More than 20 attacks on Iranian healthcare facilities since March 1: WHO
At least one killed at UAE’s Habshan gas facility after intercepted attack

What we know about the US fighter jet shot down in Iran

Red Crescent supply warehouse attacked in southwest Iran

world.headline: Trump Requests $1.5 Trillion Defence Budget, Slashing Domestic Spending
world.body:
The White House sent Congress a budget request for a historic $1.5 trillion in military spending, the largest in US history, offset by deep cuts to domestic programmes. The proposal arrives as the US wages active conflict with Iran and faces mounting legal battles at home.

Twenty-four Democrat-led states filed suit against the administration over restrictions on mail-in ballots. Separately, a federal judge upheld the dismissal of subpoenas targeting Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, handing Trump a setback in his push to pressure the central bank. US unemployment fell despite the economic turbulence of the Iran war.

sources.title: Two dozen Democrat-led states sue Trump over mail-in ballot limits
US judge upholds decision to toss subpoenas into Fed Chair Jerome Powell
US unemployment rate drops despite economic uncertainty and Iran war
Trump seeks historic $1.5 trillion for military in Congress budget request
Trump seeks $1.5tn for defence alongside domestic spending cuts

world.headline: Cuba Frees 2,000 Prisoners Under US Pressure
world.body:
Cuba began releasing more than 2,000 prisoners, a move widely seen as a direct response to sustained US diplomatic and economic pressure. The releases include political detainees held since the 2021 protests that swept the island.

The scale of the release is significant, though rights groups caution that thousands more remain imprisoned and the Cuban government has not signalled any broader political opening.

sources.title: Cuba begins releasing more than 2,000 prisoners as US pressure mounts
Cuba releases over 2,000 prisoners amid mounting US pressure

world.headline: Artemis II Crew Sends Back Stunning Earth Images
world.body:
NASA's Artemis II mission, carrying astronauts on the first crewed lunar flyby in more than 50 years, released its first photographs of Earth taken from near the moon. The images, described by the crew as "spectacular," show the full disc of Earth against deep space.

The mission marks a major milestone in NASA's push to return humans to the lunar surface, with Artemis II serving as the critical crewed test flight ahead of a planned moon landing.

sources.title: NASA releases first stunning Artemis II photos of Earth from moon mission
Artemis II crew take 'spectacular' image of Earth

ai.headline: OpenAI Reshuffles Leadership as Two Executives Take Leave
ai.body:
OpenAI is reorganizing at the top. COO Brad Lightcap moves into a new "special projects" role, while Fidji Simo, who joined to lead the company's applications division, is taking medical leave. The AGI research lead is also stepping back, leaving a notable gap in the team overseeing OpenAI's most consequential work.

The moves come as OpenAI expands aggressively on other fronts, acquiring tech talk show TBPN in what critics are calling another distraction from its core mission. The simultaneous departures and role shifts raise questions about internal stability at a company navigating a high-stakes transition from research lab to commercial giant.

sources.title: OpenAI executive shuffle includes new role for COO Brad Lightcap to lead ‘special projects’
OpenAI’s AGI boss is taking a leave of absence
OpenAI’s Fidji Simo Is Taking Medical Leave Amid an Executive Shake-Up
OpenAI takes on another "side quest," buys tech-focused talk show TBPN

ai.headline: Anthropic Buys Biotech, Launches PAC, Signals Broader Ambitions
ai.body:
Anthropic is spending big and moving fast. The AI company acquired biotech startup Coefficient Bio for roughly $400 million, a bet that AI can accelerate drug discovery and biological research. It is one of the largest moves by an AI lab into life sciences.

Separately, Anthropic launched a political action committee, formalizing its push into Washington lobbying. The dual moves - one scientific, one political - signal that Anthropic is positioning itself as more than a model provider. It wants influence over both the science and the regulation shaping the industry's future.

sources.title: Anthropic buys biotech startup Coefficient Bio in $400M deal: Reports
Anthropic ramps up its political activities with a new PAC

ai.headline: AI's Energy Hunger Drives a Natural Gas Building Boom
ai.body:
Microsoft, Meta, and Google are funding construction of large natural gas plants to power their expanding data center networks. The buildout is driven by AI workloads that demand far more electricity than conventional computing, and renewables alone cannot keep pace with the timeline these companies are working to.

The strategy carries real risks. Locking in fossil fuel infrastructure now could undermine long-term climate commitments and expose companies to regulatory and reputational blowback. A separate survey found that communities near proposed data centers are more opposed to them than to Amazon warehouses, citing noise, water use, and grid strain. The energy politics of AI are getting harder to ignore.

sources.title: AI companies are building huge natural gas plants to power data centers. What could go wrong?
People would rather have an Amazon warehouse in their backyard than a data center

ai.headline: AI Chatbots Now Prescribing Psychiatric Drugs, Raising Alarms
ai.body:
Some AI chatbot services are moving into territory that was firmly the domain of licensed clinicians: prescribing and refilling psychiatric medications. The Verge reports that patients are receiving drug recommendations and refills through AI-driven platforms with minimal human oversight.

The trend sits at the intersection of a mental health care shortage and aggressive AI product expansion. Proponents argue it widens access. Critics point out that psychiatric prescribing requires nuanced judgment about risk, history, and patient state - exactly the kind of reasoning where current AI systems fail quietly and consequentially. Regulators have not yet caught up.

sources.title: Chatbots are now prescribing psychiatric drugs

btc.headline: Circle Faces Backlash After $285M Drift Hack Goes Unfrozen
btc.body:
Circle is under mounting pressure after refusing to freeze USDC linked to a $285 million exploit of the Drift protocol. On-chain investigator ZachXBT escalated the criticism, accusing Circle of over $420 million in compliance failures dating back to 2022. The stablecoin issuer's inaction has reignited a core debate: how much control should a supposedly decentralized asset's issuer exercise when funds are stolen?

Drift responded by sending an on-chain message directly to wallets tied to the hack, a public and somewhat desperate move that underscores how limited options are when a key gatekeeper won't act. The episode puts Circle's compliance posture in an uncomfortable spotlight ahead of its anticipated IPO.

sources.title: Circle under fire after $285 million Drift hack over inaction to freeze stolen USDC
ZachXBT accuses Circle of $420M in 'compliance failures' since 2022
Drift sends onchain message to wallets tied to $280M exploit

btc.headline: Schwab Eyes Spot Crypto Trading as Bitcoin Slips Below $70K
btc.body:
Charles Schwab confirmed plans to launch spot Bitcoin and Ether trading in the first half of 2026, a move that would bring direct crypto access to millions of retail brokerage accounts. The announcement signals that traditional finance is no longer treating crypto as a fringe asset class. It matters.

The timing is awkward. Bitcoin has dipped below $70,000, and futures markets are tilting bearish. Corporate Bitcoin treasury strategies are fracturing, with some firms reconsidering their holdings as prices slide. Cathie Wood is calling the era of 85% drawdowns over, citing a new $34,000 floor target, but the market is not yet convinced. Volatility has cooled, and altcoins are outperforming BTC in the short term, suggesting rotation rather than recovery.

sources.title: Schwab plans spot bitcoin, ether trading launch in first half of 2026
Crypto Biz: Bitcoin treasuries break ranks as BTC dips below $70K
Bitcoin 'done' with 85% crashes, says Cathie Wood amid new $34K target
Crypto consolidates as volatility cools and futures markets tilt bearish
CoinDesk 20 performance update: Bitcoin (BTC) trades flat while altcoins rise

btc.headline: Coinbase Trust Charter Draws Fire From Community Banks
btc.body:
US community banks are pushing back against the OCC's approval of a trust charter for Coinbase, arguing it gives the crypto exchange an unfair regulatory foothold in traditional finance. The opposition reflects a broader turf war as crypto firms pursue banking-adjacent licenses that blur long-standing industry boundaries.

The fight is not just about Coinbase. It sits alongside Malta's clash with ESMA over crypto oversight and Kalshi's legal troubles as it hires a Democratic strategist to navigate regulatory headwinds. Across jurisdictions, the question of who controls crypto's regulatory perimeter is becoming the defining political battle of the cycle.

sources.title: US community banks oppose OCC's approval of Coinbase trust charter
Kalshi onboards ex-Democratic strategist amid legal troubles
Centralizing crypto: Why Malta’s clash with ESMA is about more than one small state

btc.headline: Ethereum Foundation Stakes $93M as L2 Scaling Debate Heats Up
btc.body:
The Ethereum Foundation completed a staking milestone, depositing another $93 million in ETH to hit its 70,000 ETH target. The move signals institutional confidence in Ethereum's staking infrastructure at a moment when the network's scaling roadmap is under scrutiny.

Offchain Labs argued this week that Ethereum Layer 2 networks need more responsive fee pricing to handle billions of users, pointing to a structural gap between current L2 design and real-world demand. Separately, a quantum-resistant blockchain from Naoris Protocol went live, citing growing concerns that advances in quantum computing could eventually threaten Ethereum and Bitcoin's cryptographic foundations. The infrastructure layer is moving fast beneath a market that looks quiet on the surface.

sources.title: Ethereum Foundation stakes another $93 million ether, reaching its 70,000 ETH target
Ethereum L2s need responsive pricing to scale, says Offchain Labs
Naoris Protocol's quantum-resistant blockchain goes live as Bitcoin and Ethereum face 'Q-Day' threats

food.name: Miso-Glazed Black Cod
food.description: Silky, caramelised fish fillets marinated in white miso, mirin, and sake then broiled until lacquered and burnished
food.tip: Marinate for at least 24 hours — the longer the miso sits on the fish, the deeper the umami crust you'll get under the grill

exercise.name: The Friday Ladder
exercise.description: Ascending and descending rep ladders alternating kettlebell swings and pull-ups (1+1, 2+2… up to 10, then back down), with no fixed rest — move when ready, stop when form breaks

learn.topic: The Ship of Theseus and Personal Identity
learn.description: The ancient paradox asks whether an object that has had all its components replaced remains fundamentally the same object — and when applied to humans, whose cells, beliefs, and memories constantly change, it becomes a sharp question about what 'you' actually are across time.
learn.why: It's a Friday, which makes it a perfect day to sit with a genuinely unsettling idea: Derek Parfit's modern take in 'Reasons and Persons' argues personal identity is less important than we think, which has radical implications for how seriously you should take your past self's commitments and your future self's interests
